Job Description


Position Overview:

We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Business Analyst to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions, ensuring successful delivery of projects and initiatives. This role requires a strong understanding of application development, system integrations, and Agile delivery environments, as well as excellent analytical and communication skills.

Key Responsibilities:


Elicit, analyze, and document business and technical requirements through stakeholder workshops and analysis.
Translate business needs into clear functional and non-functional requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
Collaborate closely with architects and developers to ensure requirements are technically feasible and well understood.
Develop and maintain key artifacts, including functional specifications, process flows, data models, and interface definitions.
Support solution design by validating technical options against business objectives and constraints.
Assist with backlog refinement, prioritization, and dependency management in Agile delivery environments.
Support testing and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) by reviewing test cases, validating outcomes, and managing requirement traceability.
Identify and manage risks, issues, and dependencies related to requirements and solution design.
Facilitate requirement walkthroughs and support stakeholder sign-off and alignment.


Required Skills & Qualifications:


Solid understanding of application development, system integrations, APIs, and data flows.
Proven experience working in Agile and hybrid delivery environments.
Strong skills in requirements documentation, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate closely with technical teams, including developers, testers, and architects.
Proficiency with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar platforms.
Exceptional stakeholder management, communication, and facilitation skills.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.


Why Join Us:

This is an exciting opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment where you will contribute to impactful projects and collaborate with talented professionals. If you are passionate about driving business success through innovative solutions and have the skills to excel in this role, we encourage you to apply.

Additional Requirements: 

Security Clearance Requirement: Given the secure nature of this role, we are seeking candidates who are Australian Citizens with an active security clearance or are eligible for obtaining security clearance.

This requirement ensures that all team members are cleared to handle sensitive information and contribute to maintaining the highest standards of security in our operations.

Benefits of working at Accenture:


18 weeks paid parental leave
Long & short-term career break opportunities
Structured career development program
Local and international career opportunities.
Certified as a Family Inclusive Workplace™
Flexible Work Arrangements - centered around Accenture’s Truly Human ethos and our commitment to supporting the health and wellbeing of our people.
We are proud to be in the top 3 of last year’s Diversity & Inclusion Index!

About the job
About William Buck

At William Buck, we know that talent and growth go hand in hand. You should be valued for what you bring and respected for where you want to go. We’re big enough to challenge the larger firms on complex work, but small enough that you’ll feel part of a team where your contributions are recognised, your voice is heard, and you’re supported by your peers.

We are more than just Accountants and Advisors – we aspire to create a positive change in the lives of our clients and our people. Every individual who works with us is recognised for who they are and given every opportunity to progress in a way that fulfils both their talents and aspirations. William Buck people enjoy better careers because we care. It’s that simple.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Business Analyst to join our Group Project Management Office (PMO) on a 12-month contract. This role will support the successful delivery of our Practice Management System (PMS) and Document Management System (DMS) transformation across multiple offices. You’ll lead requirements gathering, process mapping, solution design, and stakeholder engagement to ensure business needs are met and solutions are practical, scalable, and aligned with group strategy.

Your Responsibilities

 Analyse current and future business processes, mapping requirements and identifying improvement opportunities.
 Develop functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria for solution design.
 Facilitate workshops, interviews, and focus groups to capture and validate requirements.
 Support vendor evaluation and selection processes.
 Collaborate with project and change managers to drive adoption and minimise resistance.
 Lead user acceptance testing (UAT) and ensure solutions meet business requirements.
 Maintain clear documentation and traceability throughout the project lifecycle.

What We Are Looking For

 Business analysis certification (CBAP, CCBA) or relevant tertiary qualification.
 5–10 years’ experience in business analysis within professional services.
 Proven experience with multi-office system implementations and vendor engagement.
 Strong facilitation, documentation, and problem-solving skills.
 Familiarity with PMS/DMS ecosystems and tools such as JIRA, Planner, and PowerBI.
 Excellent communication skills and ability to influence across stakeholder groups.

The William Buck Difference

 Our commitment to your success extends beyond the role, with benefits designed to help you thrive:
 Flexible and hybrid working environment with work-life balance in mind.
 Comprehensive professional development including funded external study and memberships.
 Dedicated Career Care Coach to support your growth and progression.
 Regular social and team events including EOFY and Christmas parties, CSR opportunities, D&I events and monthly activities.

If this role sounds like a great fit, we’d love to hear from you.

At William Buck, we believe our differences make us stronger. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating an inclusive, respectful, and supportive workplace for all. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives

Please note, we are accepting applications from QLD, SA, VIC and NSW.
